parameters.jsCode: const meta=$json.metadata||{};const id=meta.identifier;const files=$json.files||[];const pick=files.find(f=>f.format==='h.264')||files.find(f=>(f.name||'').toLowerCase().endsWith('.mp4'))||files.find(f=>(f.name||'').toLowerCase().endsWith('.ogv'))||files.find(f=>(f.name||'').toLowerCase().endsWith('.m4v'))||files.find(f=>f.format==='MPEG4');if(!pick){throw new Error('No media file for '+id);}return {json:{identifier:id,title:meta.title||id,file:pick.name,format:pick.format,length:pick.length||null,mediaUrl:'https://archive.org/download/'+id+'/'+encodeURIComponent(pick.name)}};

parameters.text:
=You are analysing a diarized transcript from an archival film, to label each speaker.

FILM
Title: {{ $json.title }}
Description: {{ $json.description }}

TRANSCRIPT (speaker numbers come from automatic diarization and may be imperfect)
{{ $json.turnsText }}

TASK
For each speaker number, infer the most likely identity or role.
Rules:
- If a speaker is narrating (voiceover, main narration), label them exactly "Narrator" — never append a name or qualifier.
- Otherwise, use a real person's NAME only if the transcript or description explicitly supports it (e.g. the speaker introduces themselves by name).
- Otherwise, use a descriptive ROLE such as "Interviewer", "Interviewee", "Reporter", "Official", "Soldier", "Witness".
- If you cannot determine even a role, use exactly "Unknown speaker".
- All labels must be under 20 characters. Be concise.
- Do NOT invent or guess names. When unsure, prefer "Unknown speaker" with low confidence.
- Diarization may split one person across numbers or merge two people; note this in evidence if you suspect it.

Speaker numbers to label: {{ $json.speakerNumbers.join(', ') }}
